description: Module supports operation as Root Complex or End Point selected by strap or DEVSTAT override. Mode impacts configuration access, I/O capability, and enumeration role. Behavior is consistent across POR and software overrides when permitted. This enables flexible system topologies with a single IP. Ensure mode is set before enabling link training.
dependencies: Strap pins; Device status register access; Reset sequencing
Remarks: Set mode prior to any LTSSM enable to avoid undefined negotiation states.

description: Single link supports x1 or x2 operation at 2.5 or 5.0 GT/s per lane. Width cannot be split into two independent x1 links. Link training negotiates speed based on partner and SerDes configuration. Ensure PCB and refclk quality meet Gen2 requirements. Validate negotiated L0 parameters post-training.
evidence: PHY/SerDes description; LINK_CAP/LINK_STAT_CTRL in local config
dependencies: SerDes refclk quality; Lane routing; Partner capabilities
Remarks: Do not attempt bifurcation; only one downstream device is supported.

description: Up to four inbound regions translate PCIe BAR hits to internal bus addresses. Each region uses BAR select and START/OFFSET registers to define target mapping. Proper alignment and size constraints apply per region configuration. Configure before enabling LTSSM to accept host accesses safely. Use big-endian swap settings as needed.
dependencies: BAR sizing on EP; Internal memory map; Endianness settings
Remarks: Validate no overlap across regions and mark targets as device memory.

description: Outbound windows map internal bus addresses to PCIe address space. Index and high registers program base and size for each region. Use for MMIO/DMA style host memory access from device. Configure attributes and ordering via TLPCFG as appropriate. Ensure remote target permits requested payload sizes.
dependencies: Remote memory apertures; No-snoop/relaxed ordering policy
Remarks: Program minimal required windows to limit exposure and ease validation.

description: Configurable byte swap supports big-endian and little-endian CPU integration. Separate inbound and outbound swap behaviors are defined with documented tables. Program early in initialization to match system endianness. Verify with test transactions before functional traffic. Avoid mixed settings across regions.
dependencies: CPU endianness; Software ABI; IB/OB window programming
Remarks: Use consistent swap policy across all regions to prevent silent data corruption.

description: Link training and status state machine can be enabled and observed. Software enables LTSSM and waits for L0 state to confirm link up. State encodings are provided to aid debug of training issues. Disable LTSSM during major reconfiguration. Record transitions during bring-up for triage.
dependencies: Stable refclk; Proper SerDes settings; Partner presence
Remarks: Gate LTSSM until translations and capabilities are configured.

description: Module supports MSI reception in RC mode and MSI generation in EP mode. Multiple vectors are grouped with enable, status, and clear registers. Software must enable only used vectors and provide EOI on service completion. Configure capability structures in config space appropriately. Test with synthetic interrupts before enabling in production.
evidence: MSI*_IRQ_ENABLE_* @0x100–0x17C; MSI capability in local config
dependencies: SoC interrupt controller routing; Config space MSI capability
Remarks: Use separate ISR paths per-vector to simplify latency analysis.

description: Legacy INTA/B/C/D signaling is supported for backward compatibility. RC receives and services legacy interrupts with EOI write semantics. EP can generate legacy interrupts when MSI is unavailable. Latency and sharing constraints apply compared to MSI. Prefer MSI when supported by the host.
dependencies: SoC interrupt mapping; Host legacy INT support
Remarks: Ensure deassert/EOI ordering to prevent interrupt storms.

description: Active State Power Management states L0s and L1 are supported. Entry and exit latencies depend on link partner and SerDes tuning. Deeper L2 state is not supported on the link. Software may advertise and enable ASPM via config space controls. Validate performance impact under traffic.
dependencies: Partner ASPM capability; Clock gating policies
Remarks: Avoid enabling ASPM during early bring-up to reduce variability.

description: Module can transmit PM_TURNOFF and PME messages and report related events. Status and raw status registers expose power and reset event bits. Software should clear sticky status after handling. RC and EP roles differ in who initiates PM sequences. Logging these events aids low-power debugging.
dependencies: System power controller policies; Host OS power management
Remarks: Gate traffic and quiesce DMA-like flows before PM turnoff.

description: Root Complex can issue downstream hot reset to reinitialize link partners. Command register exposes reset trigger with flush indication. Use after error recovery or reconfiguration. Ensure graceful stop of in-flight transactions before assertion. Monitor LTSSM for recovery to L0.
dependencies: Proper quiesce of traffic; Partner reset tolerance
Remarks: Coordinate with driver stack to re-enumerate devices post-reset.

description: End-to-end CRC can be generated for outbound TLPs and checked for inbound. Enables strengthened data integrity across the link when host supports it. Configuration may be global or per-TLP attribute via capability. Balance integrity needs with performance overhead. Verify interoperability with link partner.
dependencies: Host ECRC policy; Firmware configuration sequencing
Remarks: Enable only after basic functionality is validated.

description: Outbound max payload is 128 B and inbound 256 B with remote read request size up to 256 B. Settings interact with link partner capabilities negotiated in config space. Oversized requests may be split or rejected per PCIe rules. Tune sizes for throughput and latency trade-offs. Validate under stress traffic.
dependencies: Partner advertised capabilities; Memory target alignment
Remarks: Use conservative defaults when interoperability is uncertain.

description: Software can access remote Type 0 or Type 1 configuration spaces through a programmable window. A setup register defines BDF and type prior to issuing config TLPs. Accesses must be 32-bit, single, non-burst, and non-cacheable. Use for enumeration and capability discovery. Implement back-off on completion timeouts.
dependencies: Bus topology knowledge; Non-cacheable MMIO mapping
Remarks: Avoid speculative reads; fence CPU before and after accesses.

description: Module exposes a standards-compliant local configuration space. Contents include PCI and PCIe capability structures to advertise features. Access path uses a documented offset from module base. Initialize device IDs, class codes, and BARs as required for role. Keep writes aligned and single-beat.
evidence: Local config base + 0x1000 offset; Capability list
dependencies: Proper strap-based role; System ID assignments
Remarks: Lock configuration after link-up to prevent enumeration instability.

description: In RC mode a 4 KB I/O window enables legacy I/O TLPs. Base address derives from IOBASE register high bits. Inbound I/O is not supported in any mode. Prefer MMIO when possible for performance and simplicity. Limit exposure to trusted endpoints.
dependencies: Legacy device requirements; Host firmware policy
Remarks: Do not map untrusted devices via I/O; audit accesses.

description: Transaction layer automatically manages flow control credits. Prevents oversubscription by pacing posted, non-posted, and completion traffic. Credit status affects achievable throughput under load. Monitoring via debug registers can indicate bottlenecks. Configure payload and ordering to match credit availability.
evidence: PCIe TLP handling description; DEBUG0 insights
dependencies: Partner credit advertisement; TLPCFG settings
Remarks: Benchmark with realistic traffic patterns to tune sizes.

description: The design supports only one Virtual Channel and Traffic Class. Quality of service differentiation via VC is not available. Simplifies configuration but limits advanced QoS schemes. Software should not attempt multi-VC negotiation. Rely on payload sizing and ordering for performance shaping.
dependencies: NONE
Remarks: Document limitations in system architecture to avoid false expectations.

description: PHY supports automatic lane reversal and polarity inversion for routing flexibility. This eases PCB constraints without firmware intervention in common cases. Ensure board planning aligns with supported permutations. Status can be verified via SerDes and PCS registers. Do not mix manual and automatic corrections.
dependencies: Board routing; SerDes configuration
Remarks: Validate at both Gen1 and Gen2 speeds to catch marginal paths.

description: Registers expose detailed link state and PHY indicators. LTSSM state transitions help isolate training failures. PCS status reports lane and TX/RX enables. SerDes lock and signal detect are visible at chip-level status. Capture traces during bring-up for correlation.
dependencies: Access to chip-level cfg space (kicker); Clock present
Remarks: Automate polling with timeouts and annotated state histories.

description: A defined sequence enables and locks the SerDes PLL. Timing requirements include regulator settle and lock cycle counts. Polling lock bits ensures readiness before training. Mis-sequencing leads to intermittent link failures. Follow reference timing strictly.
dependencies: Stable differential refclk; Kicker unlock; Power domain on
Remarks: Guard with retries and maximum wait to detect hardware faults.

description: The PCIe power domain must be enabled before register access. Clocks propagate after PSC or device-specific enables. Failure to power results in bus errors and undefined state. Sequence prior to SerDes and LTSSM configuration. Keep power domain on during active link.
dependencies: Platform PSC driver or baremetal PSC sequence
Remarks: Record domain transitions to correlate with link stability.

description: Chip-level configuration writes require unlocking via a kicker mechanism. This prevents accidental or malicious register changes. The sequence must precede SerDes configuration writes. Relock after completing critical setup. Store no secrets in these registers.
evidence: Device data manual: Kicker mechanism refs
dependencies: Privilege level; Secure boot policy
Remarks: Abstract via a helper to avoid leaving system unlocked inadvertently.

description: The design does not support link state L2. Attempts to enter L2 will fail or remain in prior state. System power policies must account for this limitation. Use L0s/L1 only for ASPM. Coordinate with OS to prevent unsupported transitions.
dependencies: OS power policy; Firmware configuration
Remarks: Advertise capabilities accurately in config space.

feature_name: No FLR and limited vendor messaging
description: Function Level Reset is not supported in this implementation. Vendor-specific message TLPs are not provided. Recovery must rely on hot reset or device-specific methods. Software should not expose FLR to higher layers. Plan error handling accordingly.
evidence: Capability notes (no FLR; no vendor messages)
dependencies: Error management framework; RC reset control
Remarks: Document reset latencies to meet system requirements.

description: Local and remote configuration and RC I/O windows require 32-bit single, non-burst accesses. Mappings should be marked non-cacheable to avoid coherency and ordering issues. Violations can cause protocol errors or timeouts. Apply memory barriers around these accesses. Use narrow read-modify-write patterns.
evidence: Practical notes section; CFG/IO access rules
dependencies: MMU/MPU configuration; Driver memory attributes
Remarks: Enforce via HAL APIs to prevent accidental misuse.

description: Global TLP attributes can be configured for relaxed ordering and no-snoop. These affect coherency and reordering behavior across the fabric. Use cautiously when interacting with CPU caches and DMA engines. Host support and policy dictate allowed combinations. Benchmark impact before enabling.
dependencies: Host coherency domain; Cache policies
Remarks: Default to strict ordering until validated workloads require change.

description: End-of-interrupt requires writing the specific event number to the EOI register. This acknowledges and unmasks the serviced source. Incorrect EOI handling can cause persistent interrupts. Map vector IDs carefully to handlers. Validate with nested and bursty scenarios.
dependencies: SoC interrupt controller; ISR/EOI framework
Remarks: Centralize EOI writes in a common interrupt epilogue.

description: Standard PCIe message TLPs are supported for functions like PM and MSI. These are generated or consumed based on role and capability settings. Ensure routing and ID fields are configured correctly. Validate reception paths in RC mode. Log unexpected messages for analysis.
dependencies: Correct BDF addressing; Capability enables
Remarks: Throttle message rate during bring-up to simplify debug.}]}
